src/main/java/com/example/jeed/s18/Regio.general_idea: Replace legacy List.get(0) with the new List.getFirst() method introduced in Java 21.
src/main/java/com/example/jeed/s18/Regio.why_exists: List.getFirst() is a new API in Java 21; this change is a modernization/polishment, not a fix for a compilation error (get(0) still compiles).

src/main/java/com/example/jeed/s19/CarDa.general_idea: Replace deprecated List.get(0) with List.getFirst() introduced in Java 21.
src/main/java/com/example/jeed/s19/CarDa.why_exists: List.get(0) throws IndexOutOfBoundsException if the list is empty, whereas getFirst() is the modern API equivalent; while get(0) still compiles, this change aligns with Java 21 API modernization and safety improvements.

src/main/java/com/example/jeed/s19/Servi.general_idea: Replace legacy List.get(0) with Java 21 List.getFirst() for safer access to the first element.
